data.title: Communications Services Up With Risk Appetite -- Communications Services Roundup
data.description:
Communications-services ticked up as traders rotated back into high-risk sectors in hopes of a rapid resolution to the Iran war.

Investors awaited a speech from President Donald Trump to gauge the outlook for the conflict.

Shares of Netflix, Walt Disney and other major media firms rose.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

04-01-26 1756ET

data.title: Reeves to work with supermarket bosses amid fears over food inflation
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Chancellor Rachel Reeves and supermarket bosses agreed to explore together how to ease the cost of living for consumers amid a warning that the Middle East conflict could see food inflation soar higher than 9% by the end of 2026.

The Food and Drink Federation, which represents 12,000 food and drink manufacturers, hiked its inflation forecast for the year in light of the conflict.

Reeves and Energy Secretary Ed Miliband hosted supermarket bosses, including from Tesco PLC, J Sainsbury PLC and Aldi Stores Ltd, for talks on Wednesday.

"They agreed to work together to explore what more can be done to ease the cost of living for consumers and strengthen supply chains," a government spokesperson said.

Helen Dickinson, chief executive at the British Retail Consortium, called it a "constructive" meeting and said that while "some inflation is inevitable, there are domestic policy levers that government can pull in order to mitigate some of the inflationary pressures".

It comes as economists for the FDF predicted food inflation will reach at least 9% by the end of the year, up from the 3.2% that it had forecast in September last year.

The shift has been caused by the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz and disruption and damage to energy infrastructure in the Middle East.

This has sent brent crude oil and natural gas prices skyrocketing to their highest level since 2022.

The FDF said the situation is fast-changing, but its revision to the inflation forecast is based on the assumption that the Strait of Hormuz opens to cargo traffic within the next two to three weeks and the majority of key facilities, such as oil, gas and fertiliser sites, return to normal within a year.

Disruption to oil and gas markets is having a direct and immediate impact on production costs for UK food and drink manufacturers, the FDF said.

This is because it is an industry that requires a lot of energy for the manufacturing process.

Many larger businesses are able to hedge costs by fixing energy contracts, but they are preparing for sharp price rises when contracts end, according to the FDF.

Meanwhile, it said smaller producers tend to buy energy "on the spot" and were already experiencing higher prices.

Liliana Danila, FDF's chief economist, said: "The food and drink sector is already feeling the force of this geopolitical shock.

"As one of the UK's energy intensive industries, manufacturers are facing mounting energy bills, rising transport and packaging costs and disruption across key supply chains.

"These pressures are hitting simultaneously, and are a significant challenge for businesses to absorb."

She added: "The current situation is unprecedented and hard to predict, however given the scale and speed of these cost increases, and despite companies' best efforts not to pass price increases on, it's clear that food inflation is going to rise in the months ahead."

By Anna Wise and Helen Corbett, Press Association

source: PA

Copyright 2026 Alliance News Ltd. All Rights Reserved.

data.title: Tech Up as Intel Rallies -- Tech Roundup
data.description:
Shares of technology companies rose as a bold move from Intel stirred a rally in chip makers.

Intel agreed to buy back the 49% stake in its Ireland-based microchip factory it sold to Apollo Global Management for $14.2 billion in 2024.

Chip makers were hard hit during the flight from risk triggered by the Iran war, and came storming back this week.

Anthropic is racing to contain the fallout after accidentally exposing the underlying instructions it uses to direct Claude code, the popular artificial-intelligence agent app that has won the company an edge with developers and businesses.

Manna Air Delivery raised $50 million in its Series B funding round and will use the proceeds to expand its operations in the U.S., the company's founder and chief executive officer said.

Microsoft is on track to invest $5.5 billion in cloud and AI infrastructure in Singapore through 2029, as demand for AI skills and computing continues to grow in the city-state.

A number of Baidu's Apollo Go robotaxis froze in the middle of traffic in a major Chinese city, forcing passengers to abandon the vehicles, and raising questions over the safety of self-driving cars.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

04-01-26 1752ET

data.title: Boeing Wins $900 Million Air Force Contract for T-38 Avionics Support
NATO's Rutte to visit Washington next week for 'long-planned' visit
data.description:
BRUSSELS, April 1 (Reuters) - NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte will visit Washington next week for a "long-planned" visit, a spokesperson for the military alliance said on Wednesday.

"I can confirm that the Secretary General will be in DC next week for a long-planned visit," NATO spokesperson Allison Hart said. No further details of the trip were immediately available.

The visit was first reported by the Wall Street Journal.

(Reporting by Andrew Gray; Editing by Chris Reese)

data.title: Syrian Petroleum Company announces arrival of first shipment of Iraqi fuel oil to Baniyas refinery in preparation for export to global markets
Nextera Energy says CEO John W. Ketchum's 2025 total compensation was $24.2 million - filing
Consumer Cos Up on Hopes for Brief Energy Shock -- Consumer Roundup
data.description:
Consumer companies rose amid hopes that a spike in fuel prices would be short-lived.

Retail sales rose 0.6% in February from January, beating economists' expectations, as shoppers increased spending in a broad range of categories.

Nike shares tumbled after executives predicted sales would drop in the current quarter by as much as 20% in China.

Hasbro is investigating a cyberattack that prompted the toy maker to take certain operations offline, the company said Wednesday in a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Berkeley Group shares tumbled to a nine-year low after the house builder said it was freezing new land purchases due to the gloomy economic outlook and the conflict in Iran, while its profit expectations through 2030 disappointed the market.

Food processor Conagra Brands narrowed its outlook for the year after mixed results in its fiscal third quarter, as the maker of snacks under brands such as Orville Redenbacher and Swiss Miss warned of a dynamic environment.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

04-01-26 1736ET

Health Care Up as Eli Lilly Rallies -- Health Care Roundup
data.description:
Health-care companies rose after a long-awaited regulatory approval for a weight-loss pill.

Eli Lilly shares rallied after the obesity-drug maker's once-daily anti-obesity pill received approval from U.S. drug regulators, hard on the heels of rival Novo Nordisk, which has been selling a pill version of its Wegovy since the start of this year.

German drug and chemicals maker Bayer is looking at expanding its pharmaceutical manufacturing footprint in the U.S., the head of its pharma unit said, as the business turns to the world's largest drug market to fuel a turnaround following struggles with Roundup lawsuits.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

04-01-26 1721ET

Industrials Up After Factory Data -- Industrials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of industrial and transportation companies surged after strong factory and jobs data.

The Institute for Supply Management's purchasing managers index edged up to 52.7 in March from 52.4 in February.

U.S. private employers added 62,000 workers in March, according to ADP's latest tally.

General Motors' first-quarter sales plunged, falling 9.7%, in its biggest drop in almost four years, as high interest rates and vehicle prices across the industry held buyers back even before the recent spike in gasoline prices.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

04-01-26 1717ET

Materials Up as Precious-Metal Rally Continues -- Materials Roundup
data.description:
Producers of metals, grains and other raw materials rose as a rebound in precious-metals prices continued.

Gold futures rose 2.9% to $4,783.20 a troy ounce, the fourth straight gain as fears about a potential Federal Reserve rate hike recede.

U.S. private employers added 62,000 workers in March, according to the latest tally from payroll processor ADP.

Cal-Maine shares rallied after the egg producer posted a decline in sales that was not as severe as feared, despite a drop in egg prices.

The prolonged Middle East conflict puts global fertilizer flows at risk, with about 30% of global trade concentrated in the Middle East and natural-gas feedstock originating there, said strategists at brokerage ANZ.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

04-01-26 1714ET
